title: Package Boundaries, Interfaces, Value Ownership, and Lifecycle · Spiral Systems Review
summary: A delayed operations review reconstructs package boundaries, interfaces, value ownership, and lifecycle from revision, authorization, seed, URL, robots decision, request, response bytes, tree identity, record provenance, frontier transition, goroutine owner, report row, failure, cleanup, and recovery evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for directed owned Go crawler graph after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice…
objectives: Direct policy, fetch, robots, parse, extract, frontier, audit, store, report, and command dependencies inward through consumer-owned interfaces.
Specify ownership and copy rules for URLs, headers, byte slices, DOM selections, records, errors, and report rows crossing boundaries.
Define minimal interfaces at use sites and retain concrete constructors and lifecycle ownership at composition boundaries.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

Competency probe: Wrap causal errors with operation and identity while preserving cancellation, timeout, policy rejection, retryability, and operator action.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: Formatted error strings are enough for reliable program decisions.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Wrap causal errors with operation and identity while preserving cancellation, timeout, policy rejection, retryability, and operator action. Artifact under review: directed owned Go crawler graph.

options.text: For separate evidence from confidence, crawl one convenient page, omit owner permission, revision, URL and robots policy, body and parser limits, record and graph provenance, frontier and goroutine ownership, Colly differences, changed fixtures, privacy, accessibility, recovery, and controlled native transfer, then accept plausible rows as Go crawler proof.
The record names the assumption, applies Error Taxonomy, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample. That claim does not survive the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary.

For Authorized site auditing and Go Record what required effort · Review Error Taxonomy, keep Error Taxonomy connected to executable Go authorization, URL, robots, response, tree, frontier, goroutine, report, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Wrap causal errors with operation and identity while preserving cancellation, timeout, policy rejection, retryability, and operator action.
